connectivity_tips.tip: There are many SIM Card providers in the UK. I would recommend Three. The best place to buy a sim card is at the airport kiosks. Even if you do not purchase an international roaming plan for your current India number, remember to activate roaming so that you receive messages (important for things like OTPs).
connectivity_tips._source_file: England and Scotland/Edinburgh- All Inclusive Guide.pdf

connectivity_tips.tip: eSIM-Friendly Providers good for traveling to multiple countries: Airalo, Holafly
Paper SIMs: COSMOTE. Airport kiosks often have higher prices—buy in city stores if possible
Paper SIM: COSMOTE. Airport kiosks often have higher prices—buy in city stores if possible
Paper SIMs (Local): COSMOTE. Airport kiosks often have higher prices—buy in city stores if possible

connectivity_tips.tip: Paper SIMs: Turkcell. Buy at the airport. Passport is required to activate any Turkish SIM

safety_tips.tip: Check local news or apps (like Google Alerts or Smart Traveler) for protests or strikes
Major spots like Edinburgh Castle can be busy — stay aware in pickpocket-prone areas
Major spots like Borough Market, Covent Garden, and Edinburgh Castle can be busy — stay aware in pickpocket-prone areas
safety_tips._source_file: England and Scotland/Edlyn London - All Inclusive Guide.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Keep valuables secure and out of sight in busy areas
Use a money belt or neck pouch for passports and extra cash
Carry copies of travel documents (digital and paper)
Avoid isolated alleys or beaches late at night
Beware of 'Friendship Bracelets' Scam in Monastiraki Square (Athens), Santorini viewpoints
Restaurant Price Switch - check your receipt and ask for a menu in advance
In mosques/churches: dress modestly and be respectful
Friendship Bracelets Scam - Someone tries to tie a bracelet on your wrist 'as a gift,' then demands payment. Common in Monastiraki Square (Athens), Santorini viewpoints
Restaurant Price Switch - Menus without prices or switched bills—check your receipt and ask for a menu in advance
Taxi Overcharging - Insist on the meter. Avoid taxis parked in front of touristy spots; use apps like Beat
Beware of 'Friendship Bracelets' Scam - Someone tries to tie a bracelet on your wrist 'as a gift,' then demands payment. Common in Monastiraki Square (Athens), Santorini viewpoints
safety_tips._source_file: Greece and Turkey/All Inclusive Guide - Greece and Turkey_Shital.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Restaurant Price Switch- Menus without prices or switched bills—check your receipt and ask for a menu in advance
safety_tips._source_file: Greece and Turkey/All Inclusive Guide - Greece and Turkey_Shital.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Taxi Overcharging- Insist on the meter. Avoid taxis parked in front of touristy spots; use apps like Beat
safety_tips._source_file: Greece and Turkey/All Inclusive Guide - Greece and Turkey_Shital.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Taxis without Meters - Always insist on using the meter. In Istanbul, use BiTaksi app (like Uber)
Politely decline 'Let's Have a Drink' invitations from friendly locals
Say 'no thanks' firmly to street vendors trying to place items in your hand
Let's Have a Drink Scam - A friendly local invites you to a bar—later, you're stuck with an outrageous bill. Politely decline
Street Vendor Guilt Traps - Peddlers may try to place items in your hand, then guilt you into buying. Say 'no thanks' firmly
Fake Carpet Dealers - Be cautious with 'special invites' to carpet shops claiming 'family business'
'Let's Have a Drink' Scam- A friendly local invites you to a bar—later, you're stuck with an outrageous bill. Politely decline
safety_tips._source_file: Greece and Turkey/All Inclusive Guide - Greece and Turkey_Shital.pdf

safety_tips.tip: If you visit beaches or rivers (e.g. near Windsor), follow posted advisories and avoid swimming in unsupervised areas

health_tips.tip: No vaccines are mandatory for entry, but routine vaccinations and a few travel-related vaccines are recommended
Tap water is safe to drink in most parts of Greece, but not recommended on some Greek islands like Santorini or Mykonos
Carry prescriptions in original packaging + digital copy
Tap water not recommended in many parts of Turkey. Be cautious with ice in drinks and unpeeled fruits from small vendors
Tap water is not recommended in many parts of Turkey. Be cautious with ice in drinks and unpeeled fruits from small vendors
